results.possibleIdentity: Nicholas Roukes (often misspelled as Nicholas Rouhas) is a prominent artist, art educator, and author who was highly active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. He is closely associated with the movement to integrate industrial technology and materials into fine art, a mission shared by Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.). Roukes authored several influential technical books used by artists of that era to bridge the gap between art and science, including 'Painting with Acrylics' (1966), 'Sculpture in Plastics' (1969), and 'Plastics for Kinetic Art' (1974). His publications are frequently cited alongside the work of E.A.T. and the MIT Center for Advanced Visual Studies as essential resources for artists navigating new technological media. Roukes served as a Professor of Art at the University of Calgary and specialized in themes of synectics (creativity through the joining of disparate elements) and visual humor.

Nicholas Rouhas (also known as Gust Nicholas Rouhas) is an individual who graduated from the University of Illinois in 1969 with a B.S. in Microbiology. While he was active during the specified late 1960s and early 1970s timeframe, his professional career was primarily in the field of food safety and quality assurance rather than the fine arts. He served as the western director of quality assurance for the Marriott Corporation and is mentioned in alumni records from the University of Illinois and the University of California, Santa Barbara (where a younger Nicholas Rouhas studied Film Studies in 1988).
